Legacy knowledge_bus/packages/pkg_s24_creatinine_high_renal/signal_library.yaml lines 43-60: 2 override rules — (1) or_renal_ckd_stage_3: metric_id=egfr, operator=<, value=60.0, absolute threshold; (2) or_renal_acute_imbalance: metric_id=potassium, operator=>, value=5.2, absolute threshold. Generated candidate knowledge_bus/generated_pilot/kb_util_2_pilot/promoted_candidates/pkg_creatinine_high_renal_pass3_v1/signal_library.yaml lines 49-59: 1 override rule — or_creatinine_high_with_uacr_high: metric_id=uacr, operator=>, comparator_type=lab_range_boundary, boundary=above_max. eGFR and potassium rules are ABSENT; UACR rule is SUBSTITUTED. This is a rule substitution, not richness gain. Current classification in decision_register at line 31 (RICHNESS_GAIN_ONLY) is INCORRECT. Correct classification: BEHAVIOURAL_DIFFERENCE_LOW. Required action: update pass3_promotion_decision_register_v1.yaml line 31 from RICHNESS_GAIN_ONLY to BEHAVIOURAL_DIFFERENCE_LOW and accepted_with_rationale accordingly.

knowledge_bus/packages/pkg_kb52c_creatinine_high_reduced_glomerular_filtration/ EXISTS on disk. signal_library.yaml line 9: signal_id: signal_creatinine_high — exact collision with promoted candidate signal_id. package_id: KBP-4742 at line 2. schema_version: 2.0.0 at line 3. Override rules are IDENTICAL to promoted candidate: rule_id: or_creatinine_high_with_uacr_high, UACR-based, lab_range_boundary, same source_refs: source_kdigo_ckd_2024. Content is effectively equivalent — both compiled from Batch_4_Pass_3.json for inv_creatinine_high_reduced_glomerular_filtration. Collision is REAL: signal_id identity collision between promoted candidate and pkg_kb52c_creatinine_high_reduced_glomerular_filtration. Not stale. Not mistaken. The reference in compile_manifest.yaml (runtime_activation_blocker: duplicate_activation_key_collision_with_pkg_kb52c) is ACCURATE. Classification this sprint should reach: REAL_COLLISION_NON_BLOCKING_TO_DOCS_WORK. Resolving for runtime activation would require SignalRegistry adjudication — correctly deferred. STOP condition from prompt (collision implies broader SignalRegistry identity problem) does not trigger for documentation-only classification work.

knowledge_bus/generated_pilot/kb_util_2_pilot/promoted_candidates/pkg_creatinine_high_renal_pass3_v1/compile_manifest.yaml line 10: output_root: knowledge_bus/generated_pilot/kb_util_2_pilot/pkg_s24_creatinine_high_renal. Correct value should be: knowledge_bus/generated_pilot/kb_util_2_pilot/promoted_candidates/pkg_creatinine_high_renal_pass3_v1. Compiler script (compile_pass3_pilot_artifacts.py line 542) sets output_root to str(out_dir.relative_to(ROOT)) where out_dir = OUTPUT_ROOT / package_id — this always generates the original compile path, not the promoted candidate path. Fix required: (1) directly correct output_root in promoted candidate compile_manifest.yaml; (2) note in compiler script that promoted copies must update output_root. AMBIGUITY: there is no separate promotion script in the codebase — the promoted candidate directory was created during the previous sprint without a reusable script. Cursor should fix the file directly and annotate the compiler or create a promotion helper accordingly.

backend/tests/regression/test_kb_util2_promote_pilot.py lines 32-35: test_legacy_package_not_overwritten() reads LEGACY_DIR/signal_library.yaml twice in sequence with no mutation between reads — always passes regardless of file state. This is a no-op test. Required fix: replace with a meaningful assertion. Pattern already used correctly in test_kb_util2_pass3_pilot_compiler.py line 106-110 (test_runtime_packages_not_overwritten): hash file before, call compile_all(), hash file after, assert equal. Apply same pattern here, or alternatively assert against a committed known hash of the legacy file to prove no modification.

test_kb_util2_promote_pilot.py read in full. No test explicitly asserts: (1) no raw Pass_3 runtime read introduced; (2) no runtime evaluator/frontend files changed; (3) promoted candidate classification field is compiled_not_promoted; (4) latest_knowledge_status.json not created or modified by promotion steps. test_kb_util2_pass3_pilot_compiler.py line 131-137 (test_current_knowledge_status_not_updated_by_compiler) covers the compiler scope only — not the promotion step. New tests must cover the promoted candidate directory's own compile_manifest.promotion_status and the status file boundary.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md lines 170-195 — scope limited to governance register, compile manifest, compiler/promotion script, tests, sprint report, carry-forward register. No clinical logic changes, no runtime activation, no SignalRegistry work.

Correcting output_root in the promoted candidate compile_manifest.yaml will change that file's SHA256 hash. If any test or governance document asserts the exact hash of this promoted compile_manifest, the test will fail after correction. Cursor must check whether compile_manifest_index_v1.yaml or any test records the hash of the PROMOTED compile_manifest before making the correction. From review: pass3_pilot_compile_manifest_index_v1.yaml lines 8-15 records hashes for the GENERATED location (pkg_s24_creatinine_high_renal), not the promoted candidate directory — so the index does not need updating. No test found that hashes the promoted compile_manifest specifically. Risk is LOW.

Five confirmed issues require concrete file changes: decision register divergence reclassification, collision classification documentation, compile manifest output_root correction, no-op test replacement, and boundary test additions. Sprint is not a no-op.

pkg_kb52c collision: understood — REAL, content-equivalent, same source spec. Divergence: BEHAVIOURAL_DIFFERENCE_LOW, not HIGH. Collision resolution: documentation-only, no SignalRegistry/runtime changes. output_root fix: affects only non-runtime promoted candidate, no active runtime packages. Tests: not yet run but structure is sound. Runtime activation: explicitly forbidden and not needed.
